title: A system under stress
summary:
Main
Image reference: assets/2026-03-25_a-system-under-stress_img_unsplash-image-vosp2wly3bs_5b6875639c4f.jpg
By Eirini Diamantara & Dimitris Roumeliotis
The past week has confirmed that the disruption in the Arabian Gulf is no longer just a supply shock but a market distortion that is beginning to erode demand itself. What initially supported freight through panic, dislocation and risk premiums is now evolving into something more complex: a shrinking cargo base, trapped tonnage and an increasingly fragmented tanker market. On the crude side, the numbers are becoming difficult to ignore.
